title: Unctuous German White Bread Stew
description: A slow‑cooked, glassy‑jelly German stew featuring toasted white bread, aromatic leeks, celery, and fresh herbs, crafted to be jain‑friendly and romantic.
instructions: Place kombu in a pot with 4 cups of vegetable stock; simmer 10 minutes to extract umami, then remove kombu.
Heat olive oil over medium; add sliced leek green parts and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add sliced celery and sauté 3 more minutes, then pour in the stock with kombu removed; bring to a gentle simmer.
Add thyme sprigs, lemon zest, salt, and pepper; simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Dissolve 1 teaspoon agar‑agar in 1 cup cold water; whisk until fully dissolved.
Stir agar‑agar into the pot; return to low simmer for 5 minutes to activate gelatin.
Cut bread slices into thin strips; fold into stew, reduce heat to low, cover, and let the stew slow‑cook for 80 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 80 minutes, check consistency; if too thick, add a splash of warm water; taste and adjust seasoning.
Remove thyme sprigs; stir in parsley and dill; let the stew sit 5 minutes.
Ladle the stew into bowls; garnish with dill sprigs and a light drizzle of olive oil if desired; serve hot.
